description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure unit featuring a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a metallic frame. The solar panels are dark, likely monocrystalline or polycrystalline silicon, and are supported by a visible truss structure that elevates them above a yellow and gray base. The base appears to house electrical components and possibly a battery or inverter, with some colored elements suggesting access points or indicators. The frame is constructed from metal, likely aluminum or steel, providing stability and durability. The overall design is modular and suitable for installation in urban environments such as rooftops, small parks, or utility areas. The unit is not large enough for human entry but is robust enough to support its own weight and withstand outdoor conditions.
description: A compact solar panel array mounted on a sturdy metallic frame above a yellow and gray base, designed for urban solar energy collection.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full solar panel array and the yellow-gray base with visible structural supports.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the panel array and the vertical profile of the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panels and the supporting truss structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the panel grid and the side of the base, with structural details visible.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted on a metallic frame, likely aluminum or steel
attribute_array_configuration: single row of 4 panels arranged side by side
